text: Upon entering this space, I am struck by its futuristic and slightly surreal aura. The glass walls and neon reflections evoke a sense of stepping into a place dedicated to invention and curiosity. The prominent display of "R+D" (Research and Development) on the wall affirms this. As a philosopher, I feel both anticipation and slight unease: anticipation for the human ingenuity showcased here, and unease at how technology can shape, or even dominate, the rhythms of human life. I am eager to examine how these themes unfold in this thoughtfully designed environment.
